commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Because the Scripture styles him an angel; or, because he is the immediate precursor of him whom all the prophets announced at a distance.

commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content: John, therefore, having been sent by the founder and maker of this world, how could he testify of that Light, which came down from things unspeakable and invisible? For all the heretics have decided that the Demiurge was ignorant of that Power above him, whose witness and herald John is found to be. Wherefore the Lord said that He deemed him "more than a prophet."

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content:
Applying to him the Scripture, "Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.".
Turning now to the law, which is properly ours-that is, to the Gospel-by what kind of examples are we met, until we come to definite dogmas? Behold, there immediately present themselves to us, on the threshold as it were, the two priestesses of Christian sanctity, Monogamy and Continence: one modest, in Zechariah the priest; one absolute, in John the forerunner: one appeasing God; one preaching Christ: one proclaiming a perfect priest; one exhibiting "more than a prophet"
